title: share functionality
description: due to copyright reasons we wont be able to distribute most of the actual songs we care about. but what should possible is to share the metadata around it (mainly youtube link i guess is needed for the music file itself) like the bpm, beat 1 location and beatmaps. we need some first class feature for that where people can host these as single song beatmap, single song beatmaps plural (e.g. multiple difficulties) and multi song "mixtapes". we then start extracting our existing song info we have into this modular concept and continue working from there.

title: sample-accurate editor loops
description:
Current editor looping uses the HTMLAudioElement by polling currentTime and setting audio.currentTime back to loopStart when playback reaches loopEnd. That is useful but not guaranteed to be gapless or sample-accurate. The browser may seek, decode, and resume a few milliseconds late, which can make a 1-bar loop from beat 1 to beat 5 feel uneven even when beat markers are mathematically correct.

Context from the current implementation:
- Loop markers are stored as loopMarkers.startMs and loopMarkers.endMs in src/App.tsx.
- The scheduler now treats loop end as exclusive, so a note exactly on beat 5 should not be played in a beat 1 to beat 5 loop.
- The scheduler also looks across the loop boundary so beat 1 notes can be pre-scheduled before the audio wraps.
- The remaining possible hiccup is inherent to repeatedly seeking an <audio> element, not necessarily a beat grid bug.

Goal:
Add a Web Audio based playback path for editor loop mode so practice/edit loops can be smooth and clocked by the audio engine instead of repeated JS seeks.

Likely approach:
1. Keep HTML audio for basic playback/import behavior until Web Audio is proven.
2. For editor loop playback, fetch/decode the imported audio into an AudioBuffer.
3. Use AudioContext + AudioBufferSourceNode with source.loop = true, source.loopStart = loopStartMs / 1000, and source.loopEnd = loopEndMs / 1000.
4. Track playhead using the Web Audio clock, not audio.currentTime, while Web Audio playback is active.
5. Keep projectiles/note scheduling aligned to the same clock.
6. Support play, pause, seek, restart, loop marker changes, and cleanup when changing songs/beatmaps.
7. Fall back gracefully to current HTML audio if decode fails or no loop is active.

Design concerns:
- AudioBufferSourceNode is one-shot, so pause/resume and loop marker changes require creating a new source.
- Full-song decoding increases memory use, acceptable for local desktop/editor workflow but should be handled deliberately.
- Avoid duplicating playback state in fragile ways. Prefer a small playback engine module or hook, e.g. src/audio/useSongPlayback.ts or src/audio/webAudioPlayback.ts.
- Do not regress normal song playback, import cache URLs, or recording against the timeline.

Acceptance criteria:
- Beat 1 to beat 5 loop treats beat 5 as the exclusive endpoint.
- Loop playback is audibly smoother than repeated HTML audio seeks.
- Playhead stays synced during loop playback.
- Note/projectile scheduling remains correct across the loop boundary.
- TypeScript and production build pass.

title: prepare for distribution
description: what do we need to do to be able to share the app. we need a quick start guide. optimally it could be hosted somewhere, but then where would the songs and data be stored? if hosting locally e.g. with npm run dev then we need to make sure npm i is as easy as possible. we definitely need to have finished task j7s first

title: deslop design

title: undo and redo functionality in editor.
description: use command pattern? ctrl + z and ctrl + y should work as expected. put buttons in toolbar for hints similar to 7zc

title: better use for long notes
description: we need to improve everything about notes that need to be held down. first of all there needs to be some juice when playing them, should feel a bit like grinding with a skateboard if you catch my drift, sparks or something and a feeling of something being pressed down physically. then we need the ability to manually create long notes in the editor without using record. i feel like it should work by ctrl pressing and holding (to avoid confusion with selection marquee) to be able to drag it longer (snap should be respected). when hovering over the end of a long note we need to be able to drag it left or right to extend or shorten it.

sources.content: we need to improve everything about notes that need to be held down. first of all there needs to be some juice when playing them, should feel a bit like grinding with a skateboard if you catch my drift, sparks or something and a feeling of something being pressed down physically. then we need the ability to manually create long notes in the editor without using record. i feel like it should work by ctrl pressing and holding (to avoid confusion with selection marquee) to be able to drag it longer (snap should be respected). when hovering over the end of a long note we need to be able to drag it left or right to extend or shorten it.

title: playhead activates notes when triggering them
description: for better feedback and feel even while editing it might make sense to "trigger" notes across tracks he moment the playhead touches them. that means it needs to be essential that the playhead is accurate and things feel right, like it would in a play session when the player is triggering the notes. so isntead of pads being triggered to produce some juice here the note maybe simply glows briefly or something. something suble but effective, we're still editing, not playing. might be related to 6dt?

sources.content: for better feedback and feel even while editing it might make sense to "trigger" notes across tracks he moment the playhead touches them. that means it needs to be essential that the playhead is accurate and things feel right, like it would in a play session when the player is triggering the notes. so isntead of pads being triggered to produce some juice here the note maybe simply glows briefly or something. something suble but effective, we're still editing, not playing

title: trust: ensure save and load always works as expected
description: it would be disastrous if someone worked on a track for multiple hours and saves regularly only not able to properly see their beatmap actually any more in play or edit. we need to make sure we save properly. dont accidentally overwrite stuff. maybe we need some basic backup functionality. maybe we need a way in DEbug to bust the browser cache or something

sources.content: it would be disastrous if someone worked on a track for multiple hours and saves regularly only not able to properly see their beatmap actually any more in play or edit. we need to make sure we save properly. dont accidentally overwrite stuff. maybe we need some basic backup functionality. maybe we need a way in DEbug to bust the browser cache or something
